subject: Cloud Computing
title: Containers vs. Virtual Machines
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Containers vs. Virtual Machines.

hook.text: Virtual machines are like apartments — each with its own plumbing. Containers share the building's.

buildup.text: A VM includes a full operating system. A container shares the host OS and runs only the app layer.

discovery.text: Containers start in milliseconds, use less memory, and pack more apps onto the same hardware.

twist.text: But containers share an OS kernel. One kernel bug can affect all containers on the same machine.

climax.text: Most cloud workloads use both: VMs for strong isolation, containers for speed and density.

punchline.text: VMs for safety. Containers for speed. Both have a place.

quiz.question: What is a key trade-off of containers compared to virtual machines?
options: Containers are faster but share the host OS kernel, reducing isolation
Containers are slower but provide stronger security guarantees
Containers require more memory than virtual machines
